mechanism_narrative: A sharp, unexpected increase in the unemployment rate signals a rapid deterioration in the labor market. This negatively impacts consumer confidence and future spending, leading to downward revisions of corporate earnings and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points in a single month.
evidence_refs: fact:Sharp UNRATE increases preceded market declines in 2008.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a trigger event over a 10-year lookback period.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-crisis recovery, a persistently low federal funds rate (near the zero lower bound) provides a strong tailwind for equities by keeping borrowing costs low for corporations and depressing the discount rate on future earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS has been held near zero since late 2008 to support the economy.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return of the S&P 500 is not statistically different from zero during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of slowing industrial production and accelerating inflation points to stagflationary pressures. This dynamic compresses corporate profit margins and erodes real returns, leading to poor equity performance.
evidence_refs: fact:The 1970s stagflation was characterized by poor equity returns.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 exhibits a positive average 60-day return during periods when both trigger conditions are met over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained acceleration in retail sales growth is a direct signal of robust consumer health. This translates into higher expected corporate revenues, leading to positive revisions and short-term equity market gains.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending constitutes the largest part of GDP.
failure_condition: The rolling 36-month rank correlation between the 3-month change in RSAFS and 20-day S&P 500 forward returns is consistently below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment, an upside inflation surprise raises fears of a premature monetary tightening cycle. This increases the equity risk premium and discount rates, causing a negative short-term market reaction.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL monthly % change exceeds consensus forecast by > 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:Market narrative as of 2012 is highly sensitive to Fed policy changes.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive more than 50% of the time following an inflation surprise trigger while FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A recovery in housing starts from depressed levels signals a healing housing market, which has broad positive spillovers to the financial sector, construction employment, and consumer wealth, thereby boosting equity markets.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average is greater than its 12-month moving average.
evidence_refs: fact:The 2008 financial crisis was precipitated by a housing collapse.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return is negative during periods when the trigger condition is met for at least 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Stronger than expected growth in nonfarm payrolls is a primary, timely indicator of broad economic health. This positive signal about aggregate demand and income growth drives immediate positive revisions to earnings forecasts and boosts equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:The monthly employment report is one of the most market-moving data releases.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following PAYEMS releases that beat consensus by more than 50k is negative over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth is positive and accelerating while the unemployment rate remains elevated, it signals a 'jobless recovery'. This environment is positive for equities as it implies high productivity growth and expanding profit margins without wage pressure, supported by accommodative policy.
evidence_refs: fact:The 2010-2012 period has seen GDP recover while unemployment remains high.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 fails to produce an average annualized return of at least 5% during periods when both trigger conditions are met for two or more consecutive quarters.
